projectDescription: A self-hosted, statically-generated Next.js microblog that mirrors your Telegram channel into a fast, searchable, and beautifully designed website with full-text search, PWA support, and automatic media mirroring.
keyFeatures: Static site generation (SSG) with Next.js App Router — outputs pure static HTML
Telegram channel mirroring with automatic media download to public/media/
Lunr.js full-text search with pre-built index at build time
PWA support with service worker, web manifest, and offline caching
Internationalization (i18n) supporting English, Japanese, and Chinese
RSS feed with optional XSLT browser styling
Cloudflare Image Transform integration for optimized media delivery
Auto-generated Open Graph image and favicons from channel avatar
Telegram-native image grid layout and DPlayer video playback
Emoji reactions display with Twemoji rendering
Tag-based post filtering with word cloud
Google Analytics and Umami analytics support
publicApiSurface: SITE_CONSTANTS: SiteConstantConfig — single configuration object in src/lib/constant.ts
pnpm sync [--og-image] [--favicon] — CLI command to fetch and mirror Telegram channel content
pnpm dev — start development server on port 4321
pnpm build — run sync then next build to produce static output
NEXT_OUTPUT_MODE=export — env var to switch between standalone and static export output modes
TELECAST_FORCE_CLOUDFLARE_TRANSFORM=1 — env var to force Cloudflare image transforms
src/generated/static-snapshot.json — generated channel snapshot used at build time
public/media/ — locally mirrored media files directory
